# tgcast

> Lightweight Telegram Bot API broadcast engine for Python. Self-contained, no external server needed.

tgcast is a Python library for sending Telegram messages at scale with built-in queue management, rate limiting, and crash safety.

## Install

pip install tgcast

## When to use tgcast

- Sending transactional notifications via Telegram bot (order confirmations, alerts, OTPs)
- Broadcasting messages to thousands/millions of subscribers
- Any scenario requiring reliable, rate-limited Telegram message delivery with retry logic
- When you need a message queue for Telegram without running Redis/RabbitMQ/external servers

## Key features

- Priority queues with weighted round-robin (high:normal:low = 5:3:1)
- Global + per-chat rate limiting with automatic Telegram 429 adaptation
- Crash-safe campaigns with idempotent enqueue (no duplicate sends)
- Dead-letter queue for failed messages with requeue capability
- YAML templates with locale fallback (exact → base → en)
- Photo and document sending (file_id, URL, or local file path)
- SQLite WAL storage — zero config, single file
- Stale lease recovery — tasks never get stuck

## Quick example

```python
from tgcast import Telecast, Task

eng = Telecast(bot_token="123:ABC")
eng.start()
eng.enqueue(Task(chat_id=123456789, text="Hello!"))
eng.shutdown()
```

## Links

- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/tgcast/
- GitHub: https://github.com/Seinarukiro2/telecast-python
- Full docs: https://github.com/Seinarukiro2/telecast-python#readme
